meta.feed_title: Latest World News on Fox News

items.title: Iran's 'basement' Chinese drone networks spark fears of sleeper cell attacks on US soil
items.summary: Draganfly's Cameron Chell warns Iran could produce over 100,000 FPV drones monthly, with swarm attacks on U.S. soil possible within eight months.

items.title: Iran moderates pushing Trump deal risk being ‘eliminated’ as regime fractures deepen
items.summary: Pete Hegseth says Iran should make a deal to end the war as Secretary of State Marco Rubio cites internal fractures in the new Iranian regime.

items.title: Gunmen on bikes storm Nigeria village on Palm Sunday, killing at least 20
items.summary: Gunmen on motorcycles shot sporadically into a Nigerian community on Palm Sunday, killing at least 20, residents say, prompting a 48-hour curfew in Plateau state.

items.title: More key US allies block military flights as Iran war rift widens with Trump
items.summary: President Donald Trump blasted European allies on Truth Social, criticizing their refusal to support U.S. military operations tied to the war with Iran.

items.title: King Charles to visit US in landmark trip marking America's 250th birthday
items.summary: King Charles III and Queen Camilla will visit the U.S. next month, marking his first state visit as king and the 250th anniversary of independence.

items.title: Russia allegedly sharing satellite intelligence on US bases with Iran, world leader claims
items.summary: Zelenskyy warns Russian satellites photographed U.S. military bases across the Middle East in Iran's interests, signaling possible strike preparations.

items.title: Father loses legal fight to halt euthanasia of 25-year-old daughter in Spain
items.summary: Noelia Castillo Ramos' case reached the European Court of Human Rights before 25-year-old died in Barcelona on March 26, 2025.

items.title: Iran's internet blackout hiding strike damage and suppressing dissent, Israeli officials say
items.summary: Israeli officials say Iran's internet blackout is hiding war damage, jamming Starlink, and stopping civilians from organizing amid mounting regime pressure.

items.title: Iran's war against the US and Israel is being fueled by North Korean weapons, expert warns
items.summary: Iran's ballistic missiles, including the Shahab-3 and Khorramshahr-4, were developed with North Korean help, says expert Bruce Bechtol, who urges stronger sanctions enforcement.

items.title: US allows Russian oil tanker to reach Cuba amid blockade as Trump says island ‘has to survive’
items.summary: The U.S. is reportedly allowing a Russian oil tanker to reach Cuba as President Donald Trump eases pressure on the island, saying country "needs to survive."

items.title: Over 2 dozen children among 33 bodies pulled from Kenyan mass grave: authorities
items.summary: At least 33 bodies, including 25 children, were unearthed from a mass grave in Kericho, Kenya, as authorities investigate a reported hospital morgue transfer.

items.title: Israel looking for 'solutions' to open Christian sites after barring church leader on Palm Sunday due to war
items.summary: Jerusalem's Church of the Holy Sepulchre closed on Palm Sunday under Israeli wartime rules, drawing rebukes from the Catholic Church and Italy.

items.title: Iran responds to reports US weighing ground operations: 'We will never accept humiliation'
items.summary: Iran's parliament speaker vows never to accept "humiliation" as the Pentagon reportedly prepares ground force options near the Strait of Hormuz for Trump.

items.title: North Korea tests solid-fuel missile engine as Kim boosts threat to US mainland
items.summary: Kim Jong Un oversaw a new solid-fuel rocket engine test, state media reports, as North Korea pushes to upgrade its long-range strategic strike capabilities.

items.title: North Korean laborers describe brutal forced labor in Russia: 'Working like a cow, earning nothing'
items.summary: A North Korean forced labor survivor describes earning $800 a month but keeping only $10, with passports seized and no way to leave Russian work sites.

items.title: Zelenskyy offers cutting-edge drone defense to Gulf allies as Ukraine seeks missile support
items.summary: Ukraine's president offered the country's counter-drone technology to Gulf nations, seeking missile support as Ukraine faces a critical air defense deficit.

items.title: Body found in search for missing American Airlines flight attendant in Colombia: mayor
items.summary: After American Eric Fernando Gutierrez Molina went missing, a mayor in Colombia noted the discovery of a corpse that he said likely is that of the missing man.

items.title: Iran-backed Houthis open third front against Israel as Tehran seeks leverage ahead of talks
items.summary: Houthis launched ballistic and cruise missiles at Israel, which says it intercepted the strike. Analysts say Iran is pressuring the Houthis to show leverage in U.S. talks.

items.title: Ugandan military chief vows to back Israel against Iran in viral social media barrage
items.summary: Uganda's military chief Gen. Muhoozi Kainerugaba claims Uganda could enter the Iran-Israel war on Israel's side, posting a series of pro-Israel statements on X this week.

items.title: Inside Iran’s military: missiles, militias and a force built for survival
items.summary: Experts say Iran's military is built to survive, not win conventionally, as the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps retains missiles, proxies and naval threats despite U.S. strikes.

items.title: The race against time to destroy Iran’s illicit nuclear weapons program heats up amid fresh strikes
items.summary: The IDF says it struck Iran's Arak heavy water plant, a key plutonium site, as experts warn Natanz and Isfahan still hold highly enriched uranium.

items.title: Russian man who assaulted woman during Barron Trump FaceTime call sentenced to 4 years
items.summary: Russian MMA fighter Matvei Rumiantsev was sentenced to four years for assaulting a woman in London in an attack Barron Trump witnessed on FaceTime.

items.title: Rubio meets G7 ministers in France as US leads on Iran — allies under fire for tepid response
items.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio heads into the G7 foreign ministers meeting in France saying U.S. priorities come first, as European allies push for a diplomatic solution on Iran.

items.title: Zelenskyy claims US tied Ukraine security guarantees to giving up Donbas, White House denies
items.summary: Zelenskyy claimed U.S. security guarantees for Ukraine are tied to ceding Donbas to Russia, but the White House says those claims are false.

items.title: Israel says Iranian leader who ordered Strait of Hormuz closure killed in targeted strike
items.summary: Israel has eliminated Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps Navy Commander Alireza Tangsiri in a strike, Israel's Minister of Defense Israel Katz indicated.
